nodes.label: Hub-Skill Routing: plugin.json declares hubs only; sub-skills discovered via routing trees using ../ relative links — eliminates flat-list ambiguity
Long-Running Workflow Protocol (PROGRESS.md + incremental git commits)
Quality Gate Enhancers (append official plugin agents to any team)
Three Adversarial Patterns (Reviewer 2, Stepwise Derivation, Instrument Margin)
v3.4.0 Research-Suite Extraction from Science-Suite: research-expert + 5 methodology skills split out so science-suite stays purely computational
Style Enforcement via style_lint.py (no em dashes, no banned vocabulary, quantified language)
Skill Size Governance (>3000 bytes = review required, >80% = at-risk, >90% = refactor)
Version Single Source of Truth in plugin.json
MCP Server: Sequential Thinking (structured multi-step analysis)
Scientific Review Mode Flag Implementation Plan
Scientific Review Mode Flag Design Spec
Hub-Skill Two-Tier Architecture
nodes.rationale: Since v3.1.0, skills use a two-tier hub-skill architecture where hub skills route to specialized sub-skills via a Routing Decision Tree, reducing plugin.json complexity and enabling composable skill discovery.

nodes.label: scientific-review --mode flag design pattern
nodes.rationale: The --mode flag (simple/standard/comprehensive) gates reference file loading to reduce token consumption by 39-85%. SKILL.md becomes a lean always-loaded base; reference files are conditionally loaded based on mode. Default is standard. This avoids loading all 28,971 bytes on every invocation when most reviews don't need the full report template and integrity checklist simultaneously.

nodes.label: Cross-Suite Agent Teams Pattern
nodes.rationale: Teams 13-16 and 22-25 are specifically designed for cross-suite collaboration, bridging agents from dev-suite, science-suite, and research-suite to address performance optimization, HPC interoperability, and reproducible research workflows.

nodes.label: Strangler Fig Migration Pattern
nodes.rationale: Legacy modernization uses the Strangler Fig pattern: incrementally replace the old system with the new one while maintaining backward compatibility, validated at each phase with /double-check.

nodes.label: Quality Gate Enhancers Pattern
nodes.rationale: Official plugin agents (pr-review-toolkit, feature-dev, plugin-dev) can be added to any existing team as quality gates alongside MyClaude domain experts, using the Quality Gate Enhancers pattern documented in the Agent Teams Guide.

nodes.label: Model Tier: opus
nodes.rationale: Opus is used for deep reasoning, architecture, and research tasks. Assigned to orchestrator, reasoning-engine, context-specialist, software-architect, debugger-pro, neural-network-master, nonlinear-dynamics-expert, research-expert, research-spark-orchestrator, simulation-expert, statistical-physicist (11 agents total).

nodes.label: Model Tier: sonnet
nodes.rationale: Sonnet is used for standard development and analysis tasks. Assigned to 13 agents: app-developer, automation-engineer, devops-architect, quality-specialist, sre-expert, systems-engineer, ai-engineer, jax-pro, julia-ml-hpc, julia-pro, ml-expert, prompt-engineer, python-pro.

nodes.label: Model Tier: haiku
nodes.rationale: Haiku is used for fast, simple tasks. Only assigned to documentation-expert (1 agent).

hyperedges.label: Research-Spark Orchestrator Cross-Suite Fan-Out: orchestrator delegates Stage 6 JAX/Julia/MD to science-suite agents
Hub-Skill Routing Pattern: plugin.json → hub SKILL.md → routing tree → sub-skill
sci-compute team covers all science agents across 7 variants (bayesian, julia-sciml, julia-ml, dynamics, md-sim, desktop, reproduce)
CI/CD Pipeline Setup: automation-engineer + ci-cd-pipelines hub + workflow-automate command
Bayesian Inference Pipeline: jax-pro + bayesian-inference hub + jax-computing hub
scientific-review --mode flag: SKILL.md + review_structure.md + integrity_checks.md conditionally loaded
